disclaimer: Sample analyst notes bundled as offline fixture data. Replace with a licensed game-notes provider before production use.

notes.title: Offensive identity check: early downs (sample)
notes.body: The cleanest tell for this offense is early downs: when first-down runs and quick game keep the offense ahead of the sticks, the whole call sheet opens up. Watch success rate on first and second down, not raw yardage. If Texas is living in third-and-long against the better fronts on the schedule, the explosiveness numbers will not save the drive count. Sample analyst note captured July 1, 2026.

notes.title: Roster context: interior offensive line rotation (sample)
notes.body: The roster context is still thin at interior offensive line, where the two-deep is unsettled and the staff has rotated combinations at both guard spots through spring. Until the rotation hardens, treat any line-of-scrimmage projection as provisional: pad-level questions inside show up first on short yardage and in pass protection versus interior pressure. Sample analyst note captured July 1, 2026.

notes.title: Quarterback operation and clean-game markers (sample)
notes.body: The quarterback room's job in September is a clean operation: on-time snaps, checks that keep runs out of loaded boxes, and zero gift turnovers in plus territory. Field position is the quiet stat to track; a punt team and a quarterback who both avoid disasters are worth more than a highlight throw in the opener window. Sample analyst note captured July 1, 2026.

notes.title: Defensive front seven: pressure without blitzing (sample)
notes.body: The front seven's ceiling question is pressure with four: if the interior can win one-on-ones, the coverage shell stays intact and explosive passes drop. Watch pressure rate on early downs and how often the staff has to send five or more to move the quarterback off his spot. Run fits on duo and counter are the other early tell. Sample analyst note captured July 1, 2026.

notes.title: Opponent scan: Ohio State week two (sample)
notes.body: Ohio State in week two is the schedule's first real line-of-scrimmage test: an experienced interior defensive line and receivers who punish busted coverage. The football read is whether Texas can stay on schedule on early downs and protect without chipping on every snap; if the opener vs Texas State shows a clean operation, this matchup is where the evidence gets stress-tested. Sample analyst note captured July 1, 2026.

notes.title: Opponent scan: Texas State opener (sample)
notes.body: Texas State is an opener built for calibration, not drama: a tempo offense that will test tackling angles and a front that should lose the line of scrimmage. Treat the game as a baseline read on early-down efficiency, personnel packages the staff actually trusts, and how quickly the second unit gets meaningful snaps. Sample analyst note captured July 1, 2026.
